description: Fix browser_execute_script to work on strict-CSP pages (like GitHub) by eliminating the new Function() eval that CSP blocks, and add comprehensive E2E tests covering CSP scenarios to prevent regressions.

userStories.title: Replace new Function() with inline code in the exec file wrapper to bypass CSP
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want browser_execute_script to work on pages with strict Content Security Policy (e.g., GitHub with script-src that disallows unsafe-eval) so that I can execute arbitrary JavaScript on any page regardless of CSP restrictions.
acceptanceCriteria: The writeExecFile function in adapter-files.ts no longer uses new Function() to wrap user code
User code is placed inline in the wrapper IIFE body instead of being passed through new Function()
The wrapper still captures sync results, async (Promise) results, and errors into namespaced globalThis.__openTabs keys
The wrapper still prevents user code from breaking the IIFE structure (no wrapper breakout attacks)
The exec file is still a valid .js file that chrome.scripting.executeScript({ files }) can inject
All existing browser_execute_script E2E tests in e2e/browser-tools.e2e.ts still pass (return types, DOM access, async, errors, cleanup, concurrency, truncation)
All existing browser_execute_script unit tests in platform/mcp-server/src/browser-tools/execute-script.test.ts still pass
All existing extension-side unit tests in platform/browser-extension/src/browser-commands/extension-commands.test.ts still pass
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes

userStories.title: Add E2E tests for browser_execute_script on strict-CSP pages
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want E2E tests that verify browser_execute_script works on pages with strict Content Security Policy so that CSP regressions are caught immediately and never reach production.
acceptanceCriteria: A new test.describe block in e2e/strict-csp.e2e.ts (or a new dedicated file) tests browser_execute_script on the strict-CSP test server
Test: sync code execution returns correct result on strict-CSP page (e.g., return document.title)
Test: DOM access works on strict-CSP page (e.g., querySelectorAll count)
Test: async code (Promise) resolves correctly on strict-CSP page
Test: code that throws returns an error object on strict-CSP page (not a CSP violation)
Test: code with no return produces null on strict-CSP page
Test: sequential executions on strict-CSP page leave no leftover globalThis state
Test: concurrent executions on strict-CSP page do not collide
Test: the strict-CSP test page actually enforces CSP (verify that eval() or new Function() would fail if attempted directly — this validates the test environment is truly strict)
All tests pass with the CSP fix from US-001 applied
All existing strict-CSP E2E tests still pass
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
npm run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
CONTEXT: The existing strict-CSP E2E tests (e2e/strict-csp.e2e.ts, 811 lines) test plugin adapter injection and plugin tool dispatch on strict-CSP pages, but they NEVER test browser_execute_script. The browser_execute_script E2E tests (e2e/browser-tools.e2e.ts, lines 382-831) only run against the regular test server with no CSP. This gap is exactly what allowed the new Function() CSP bug to exist undetected.

WHERE TO ADD TESTS: Add a new test.describe('browser_execute_script on strict-CSP page') block to e2e/strict-csp.e2e.ts. The strict-CSP test server (e2e/strict-csp-test-server.ts) already exists and applies `Content-Security-Policy: script-src 'none'; default-src 'none'; connect-src 'self'` headers. The existing test fixtures already provide `strictCspServer` fixture.

TEST INFRASTRUCTURE:
- Use the same pattern as existing strict-CSP tests: start strictCspServer, open a tab to it, call browser_execute_script via mcpClient.callTool()
- The existing helpers like openTestAppTab (e2e/helpers.ts) work with any test server URL — pass the strictCspServer URL
- Use parseToolResult from e2e/helpers.ts to parse responses
- For the 'CSP enforcement verification' test, use browser_execute_script itself to try `eval('1+1')` or `new Function('return 1')()` and verify it would fail (this validates the test environment)

TEST PATTERNS TO MIRROR: The new tests should mirror the patterns in the existing browser_execute_script suite (e2e/browser-tools.e2e.ts lines 382-831) but run against the strict-CSP server instead of the regular test server. Key tests to replicate:
1. Return types (string from document.title)
2. DOM access (querySelectorAll count)
3. Async/Promise resolution
4. Error throwing (verify the error is from the user code, not CSP)
5. No-return produces null
6. Sequential cleanup (no leftover __execResult_ keys)
7. Concurrent non-collision (Promise.all on the same strict-CSP tab)

CRITICAL TEST — CSP enforcement verification:
Add a test that proves the strict-CSP page actually blocks eval-like constructs. Use browser_execute_script to run code that tries `eval('1')` — this should throw a CSP error. Then run normal code like `return 1+1` and verify it succeeds. This proves that our inline approach works while eval would fail. This test is the canary — if anyone reintroduces new Function() in the wrapper, this test will catch it.

EXISTING FIXTURE REFERENCE:
- e2e/fixtures.ts: startStrictCspServer() returns a TestServer with .port and .url
- e2e/strict-csp.e2e.ts line 82 shows the pattern: fixtureTest('test name', async ({ mcpServer, strictCspServer, extensionContext, mcpClient }) => { ... })
- e2e/helpers.ts: openTestAppTab() opens a tab and waits for it to load
- The strict-CSP server serves at /strict-csp path — check strict-csp-test-server.ts for exact routes

IMPORTANT: When opening a tab to the strict-CSP server, browser_execute_script won't work for the initial readyState poll (since the whole point is CSP was blocking it before the fix). Use browser_get_tab_info or a simple delay + browser_get_tab_content to verify the tab has loaded before running execute_script tests. Or, since US-001 fixes the CSP issue, execute_script polling should work — but be defensive in the test setup.

userStories.title: Update build-plugin skill documentation to remove incorrect CSP warning about browser_execute_script
userStories.description: As a plugin developer reading the build-plugin skill documentation, I want accurate information about browser_execute_script and CSP so that I don't avoid using the tool unnecessarily on strict-CSP pages.
acceptanceCriteria: The build-plugin skill file (.claude/skills/build-plugin/__SKILL__.md) no longer warns that CSP may block browser_execute_script
The gotcha about CSP blocking browser_execute_script is updated to state that the tool bypasses page CSP via file-based injection
The auth discovery section no longer recommends avoiding browser_execute_script on CSP-strict pages
The documentation accurately reflects the post-fix behavior: browser_execute_script works on all pages regardless of CSP
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run knip passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
This is a documentation-only change. The build-plugin skill file at .claude/skills/build-plugin/__SKILL__.md has two sections that incorrectly warn about CSP blocking browser_execute_script:

1. Lines ~750 (Gotcha #11): 'CSP may block browser_execute_script — Sites with strict CSP (like GitHub: script-src github.githubassets.com) block eval/inline scripts. browser_execute_script runs in the MAIN world and is subject to the page's CSP.' — This was true before the fix but is now incorrect. Update to say browser_execute_script bypasses page CSP via file-based injection and works on all pages.

2. Lines ~138-142 (Auth discovery): 'If CSP blocks browser_execute_script (check for script-src that doesn't include unsafe-eval), use DOM-based exploration' — Remove or update this conditional since browser_execute_script now works regardless of CSP.

Keep the note that 'The adapter IIFE itself bypasses CSP because it's injected as a file URL' — this is still accurate and applies to both adapters and browser_execute_script.

Also update the module-level docstring in the skill file if it references CSP limitations for execute_script.
